what_it_does: Records verifiable completion criteria, build status, and verification evidence for the Universal Projection instrument/engine, mixing a static truth-table with a chronological, evidence-heavy build diary.
observations: FUNCTION must be verified by use (run, curl, browser); FORM must be judged by a separate design-critic and machine-gated by design-lint.
Status uses emojis: ✅ verified-by-use, 🟡 designed/stubbed, 🔴 broken/absent.
The 'floor' was corrected: the instrument AUTHORS, it is not read-only; no surprise writes; no autonomous self-modifying builds; safety = git-revert.
Registry-is-truth principle: lenses, bindings, and sectors are declared rows, never hardcoded.
Chronological entries include git commit hashes, live verification details, acceptance counts, and scope-honesty corrections.
Major seams documented: space-as-variable, recall→project()→resolve(), first-slice DOM mount, gallery render, talk/annotate hooks, constellation data path.
A legibility gap is identified as the root BAR failure: Tim cannot read the surface; meaning must live in self-describing registry fields, not in the instrument.
Binary quantization (#1) is built and verified live; Postgres (#2) is blocked on infra/Tim input.
G10 edge-coverage audit shows cascade-precedence built with real data; relation_types remain data-gated.
Re-verification acceptance battery totals are given (e.g., 211 checks / 6 suites).
standouts: Strict 'verified-by-use' epistemology that rejects reading code or lint-only as proof.
The corrected floor: instrument authors, no autonomous self-modifying builds, safety through revert.
Registry-is-truth + everything-is-a-variable as core architectural principles.
Legibility reframed as a property of memory/data, not UI labels.
Evidence-rich, hash-tagged, timestamped build diary with explicit overclaim corrections.
conventions: Markdown headers, emoji status markers, timestamped entries, and inline git commit hashes.
Heavy use of bold for claims and status.
Mix of specification, verification log, and first-person narrative.
Attribution to Tim Geldard at the top.
concerns: File is very long and truncated in the excerpt; completeness and currentness are unclear.
Dense jargon and many cross-references make it hard to read in isolation.
Mix of durable criteria and ephemeral daily status may obscure the stable completion criteria.
Some sections contain corrected overclaims, signaling prior instability in claims.
notes: A living, evidence-heavy completion log for the Universal Projection instrument that enforces verified-by-use proof and registry-is-truth design, currently centered on legibility and operator-surface gaps.
questions: What is the exact scope of the 'operator surface + right-hand-man' commission versus this file?
Which acceptance suites and counts map to which criteria groups?
How are the proposed self-describing registry fields intended to be authored and backfilled?
What is the precise relationship between this file and OPERATOR-SURFACE-LOOP.md, STATUS.md, and EQUATION-AUDIT.md?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a truth-table of verifiable statements; actual content is also a chronological build/status diary with narrative evidence. It mostly matches but goes beyond a static truth-table.
apparent_themes: Everything is a variable / instrument authors
No churn / honest stand-down when blocked
intention_signals: To create a rigorous, self-policing record that the Universal Projection build is complete only when independently verifiable by use, while surfacing and holding on gaps rather than green-painting them.
novelty: Explicit 'verified-by-use' rule, the 'instrument authors / no autonomous self-modifying builds' floor, and treating legibility as self-describing memory fields rather than UI labels.
contribution: A file-local, evidence-rich completion criteria and verification diary that preserves the build's epistemic standards, status corrections, and open gaps for a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: COMPLETION-CRITERIA.md is a verifiable-statement truth-table and chronological build log for the Universal Projection instrument, defining verified-by-use and design-critic acceptance rules and recording which features meet the bar, which are stubbed or broken, and which gaps are held awaiting input.
intention_for_embedding: To keep the Universal Projection build honest by recording only verifiable claims, rejecting code-reading as proof, and surfacing blocked work rather than churning.
